title: Systems and methods for depositing material in a patient
abstract: A system for treating a medical condition comprises a harvesting device, a processing device, and a depositing device. The harvesting device harvests tissue from a mammalian subject at a harvest site. The processing device processes the harvest tissue. The depositing device deposits material in a patient at a deposit site, the material based on the harvested and processed tissue. The deposited material is configured to generate resultant tissue configured to treat the medical condition of the patient. Methods are also provided, the methods including harvesting and processing tissue, and treating a patient by depositing material in the patient, the material being based on the harvested and processed tissue.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A system for treating a medical condition, comprising: a harvesting device for harvesting tissue from a mammalian subject at a harvest site; a processing device for processing the harvest tissue; a depositing device for depositing material in a patient at a deposit site, the material based on the harvested and processed tissue; and wherein the deposited material is configured to generate resultant tissue configured to treat the medical condition of the patient. 2. The system as claimed in at least one of the preceding claims, wherein the harvesting device is configured to perform an inside-out biopsy. 3. The system as claimed in at least one of the preceding claims, wherein the processing device is configured to process the tissue. 4. The system as claimed in at least one of the preceding claims, further comprising a treatment device for treating tissue. 5. The system according to claim 4, wherein the treatment device treats tissue at the deposit site and/or tissue proximate the deposit site. 6. The system according to claim 5, wherein the treatment device treats tissue prior to, during, and/or after deposit of the material at the deposit site. 7. The system according to claim 4, wherein the treatment device is configured to perform a tissue expansion procedure, wherein the treatment device is configured to introduce a fluid into tissue proximate the deposit site. 8. The system according to claim 7, wherein the fluid comprises saline. 9. The system according to claim 7, wherein the treatment device is configured to introduce the fluid into the tissue prior to, during, and/or after deposit of the material at the deposit site. 10. The system according to claim 7, wherein the tissue expansion procedure is performed to enhance distribution of the material at the deposit site. 11. The system according to claim 7, wherein the treatment device introduces the fluid comprising a volume of at least lmL, 5mL, lOmL, 15mL, or 20mL. 12. The system according to claim 7, wherein the fluid includes a visualizable agent selected from the group consisting of: an agent visualizable by a visible light camera, such as methylene blue; a fluorescent agent; an agent visualizable by an infrared camera; an agent visualizable by ultrasound; an agent radiographically visualizable; and combinations thereof. 13. The system according to claim 4, wherein the treatment device includes one, two, three, or more fluid delivery elements. 14. The system according to claim 13, wherein the fluid delivery elements comprise needles. 15. The system according to claim 13, wherein the fluid delivery elements comprise waterjets. 16. The system according to claim 13, wherein the fluid delivery elements are configured to deliver an expansion fluid. 17. The system according to claim 13, wherein the fluid delivery elements are configured to deliver the material. 18. The system according to claim 13, wherein the fluid delivery elements are configured to deliver both an expansion fluid and the material. 19. The system according to claim 18, wherein the fluid delivery elements are configured to deliver the expansion fluid and subsequently deliver the material. 20. The system according to claim 4, wherein the treatment device is configured to expand two or more axial and/or circumferential segments of tissue at the deposit site. 21. The system according to claim 20, wherein the material is subsequently deposited at the expanded segments. 22. The system as claimed in at least one of the preceding claims, wherein the depositing device further comprises a treatment device configured to treat tissue proximate the deposit site. 23. The system according to claim 22, wherein the depositing device is configured to both treat tissue proximate the depositing device and to deliver the material to the deposit site. 24. The system as claimed in at least one of the preceding claims, further comprising an access device for introducing at least the harvesting device into the mammalian subject. 25 The system as claimed in at least one of the preceding claims, further comprising an agent configured to be delivered to the patient and/or the mammalian subject. 26. The system as claimed in at least one of the preceding claims, further comprising a diagnostic kit including one or more components configured to perform an analysis. 27. The system according to claim 26, wherein the diagnostic device is configured to perform an analysis of a first patient and/or a second patient. 28. The system according to claim 27, wherein the diagnostic device is configured to perform an analysis of first patient and/or second patient tissue. 29. The system according to claim 26, wherein the diagnostic device is configured to perform an analysis of the material. 30. The system according to claim 26, wherein the diagnostic kit includes an endoscope, wherein the endoscope is configured to screen the patient for cancer, infection, and/or other gastrointestinal pathology. 31. The system according to claim 30, wherein the patient is excluded from treatment upon 

 the identification of cancer, infection, and/or other gastrointestinal pathology. 32. The system according to claim 26, wherein the diagnostic kit includes components to perform a tissue test on the tissue. 33. The system according to claim 32, wherein the tissue test is configured to confirm the absence of a characteristic selected from the group consisting of: infected tissue; undesired bacteria; endotoxins and/or other toxins; cancerous tissue; mycoplasma; undesired proteins, such as GIP, GLP-l, and/or other incretins, and such as a test including a response to a glucose-based stimulus; a virus; undesired bacteria; E. coli; an adventitious agent; other undesirable tissue characteristics; and combinations thereof. 34. The system according to claim 32, wherein the tissue test is configured to screen for a condition selected from the group consisting of: cancer, such as a cancer of the GI tract; an infection, such as an infection of the GI tract; presence of clostridium difficile bacteria (C. difficile); HIV; Hepatitis virus A, B, and/or C; syphilis; tuberculosis; and combinations thereof. 35. The system according to claim 32, wherein the tissue test is configured to confirm the tissue is from a particular donor. 36. The system according to claim 32, wherein the tissue test is configured to confirm the presence of a desired material, such as a material selected from the group consisting of: desired proteins, such as GIP, GLP-l, and/or other incretins, and such as a test including a response to a glucose-based stimulus; immune cells; stem cells; enteroendocrine cells; a genetic sequence, such as an mRNA expression; cell surface antigens; and combinations thereof. 37. The system according to claim 32, wherein the tissue test comprises a donor confirmation test comprising a test selected from the group consisting of: DNA test; mRNA assay; proteomics assay; flow cytometry assay; immunohistochemical analysis; enzyme-linked immunosorbent assay (ELISA); and combinations thereof. 38. The system according to claim 26, wherein the diagnostic kit is configured to assess a parameter related to an expansion of tissue. 39. The system according to claim 26, wherein the diagnostic kit is configured to assess a quantity of tissue. 40. The system according to claim 39, wherein the quantity comprises a quantity of cells. 41. The system according to claim 26, wherein the diagnostic kit is configured to assess a concentration and/or ratio of one or more substances of tissue. 42. The system according to claim 26, wherein the diagnostic kit is configured to assess a parameter during and/or after the tissue is expanded. 43. The system according to claim 42, wherein the diagnostic kit is configured to confirm an adequate quantity of the material and/or confirm other expansion parameters selected from the group consisting of: cell growth rate; organoid growth rate; organoid density, such as within a growth substrate; morphometry of organoids, including a quantification of the number of buds and/or crypts in the organoids; cell culture media secretions, such as GIP, GLP-l, insulin, and/or other marker peptide not normally secreted by this cell type; and combinations thereof. 44. The system according to claim 26, wherein the diagnostic kit includes components to assess a parameter selected from the group consisting of: number of crypts in a tissue sample; basement membrane matrix seeding density, such as derived from crypt count; presence and/or concentration of immune cells; fraction of Lgr5+ cells relative to other cell types; spatial distribution of cells, such as distribution of cells in an organoid, such as distribution of stem cells at the ends of crypts buds, Paneth cells immediately adjacent to Lgr5+ stem cells, and differentiated cells clustered near the central cystic area of an organoid; and combinations thereof. 45. The system according to claim 26, wherein the diagnostic kit is configured to perform one or more tests to determine successful modification of cells in tissue. 46. The system according to claim 45, wherein the one or more tests are selected from the group consisting of: PCR-based test; reporter proteins test, such as an eGFP test; his- tagging test, such as a reporter protein and/or of an otherwise functional protein of interest; antibiotic selection test, such as a test for resistance to puromycin; a test for 

 modified surface and/or transmembrane proteins; and combinations thereof. 47. The system according to claim 26, wherein the diagnostic kit is configured to perform one or more tests on byproducts produced during the processing of the tissue and/or the material. 48. The system according to claim 26, wherein the diagnostic kit is configured to perform a test to determine safety and/or efficacy of the material prior to deposit in the patient. 49. The system according to claim 48, wherein diagnostic kit is configured to confirm a parameter selected from the group consisting of: endotoxin and/or other toxin levels are below a threshold; bioburden is below a threshold; mycoplasma level below a threshold; adventitious agent below a threshold; cell viability above a threshold, such as above a threshold of 70%; percentage of Lgr5+ cells above a threshold, such as above a threshold of 1%, 2%, or 30%; percentage of Paneth cells above a threshold, such as above a threshold of 1%, 2%, or 30%; transduction copies per cell above a threshold; potency above a threshold; and combinations thereof. 50. The system according to claim 26, wherein the diagnostic kit is configured to assess potency of the material 51. The system according to claim 50, wherein the potency assessment comprises a quantified expression of incretins and/or an expression ratio of one incretin to another. 52. The system according to claim 51, wherein the potency assessment comprises expressions compared to a threshold to determine adequacy of the material. 53. The system according to claim 50, wherein the potency assessment comprises a quantification of the number of cells, crypts, and/or organoids in the material. 54. The system according to claim 26, wherein the diagnostic kit is configured to provide identity information of the material. 55. The system according to claim 54, wherein the information comprises anatomical location information. 56. The system according to claim 54, wherein the diagnostic kit is configured to quantify and/or detect markers of source tissue from cells other than enteroendocrine cells. 57. The system according to claim 26, wherein the diagnostic kit is configured to perform a blood test, such as a blood test of a first patient and/or a second patient. 58. The system according to claim 57, wherein the blood test evaluates the patient suitableness for the treatment. 59. The system according to claim 57, wherein the blood test evaluates circulating levels of hormones, presence of particular antibodies, and/or blood borne markers. 60. The system as claimed in at least one of the preceding claims, further comprising a storage kit including one or more components configured to store the tissue and/or material. 61. The system according to claim 60, wherein the storage kit comprises one or more containers that includes a unique identification. 62. The system according to claim 60, wherein the storage kit comprises one or more environmentally controlled containers. 63. The system according to claim 60, wherein the storage kit includes one or more cleaning and/or other agents to wash the tissue and/or material. 64. The system according to claim 63, wherein the storage kit comprises a washing agent selected from the group consisting of: surfactant; detergent, such as Triton X-100 or SDS; buffered saline; and combinations thereof. 65. The system according to claim 60, wherein the storage kit includes one or more storage solutions. 66. The system according to claim 65, wherein the storage solution comprises a preservative selected from the group consisting of: a preservative configured to arrest cellular apoptosis; a Rho kinase inhibitor, such as Y27632, and such as at a concentration of 5uM to l5uM; an antimicrobial reagent, such as Primocin, and such as at a 0.1% to 0.3% v/v solution; dimethyl sulfoxide (DMSO), such as at a 10% v/v solution, and such as for cryopreserved shipment; and combinations thereof. 67. The system as claimed in at least one of the preceding claims, further comprising a safety assembly including one or more components configured to assure the safety and/or efficacy of the material prior to its deposit in the patient. 68. The system according to claim 67, wherein the safety assembly is configured to confirm the tissue and/or material is not exposed to an undesired temperature, such as exposure to high or low temperatures for an undesired amount of time. 69. The system according to claim 67, wherein the safety assembly is configured to confirm the tissue and/or material is not exposed to an undesired pressure. 70. The system according to claim 67, wherein the safety assembly is configured to confirm the tissue and/or material is not exposed to an undesired force. 71. The system according to claim 67, wherein the safety assembly is configured to confirm the tissue and/or material is not exposed to an undesired pH level. 72. The system according to claim 67, wherein the safety assembly is configured to confirm the tissue and/or material is not exposed to an undesired physical state, wherein the safety assembly comprises one or more sensors selected from the group consisting of: temperature; pressure; force; pH; and combinations thereof. 73. The system according to claim 72, wherein the one or more sensors are configured to accompany the tissue and/or material during storage and/or transportation. 74. The system according to claim 67, wherein the safety assembly comprises one or more components configured to destroy the material if an adverse condition is detected. 75. The system according to claim 74, wherein the safety assembly includes a portion of a tissue modification kit, the portion comprising genetic material incorporated into the material, wherein the genetic material is configured to cause death of a cell within the material when the adverse condition is detected. 76. The system as claimed in at least one of the preceding claims, further comprising an identification kit including one or more components configured to identify the tissue and/or material prior its deposit in the patient. 77. The system as claimed in at least one of the preceding claims, further comprising a tissue expansion kit including one or more components configured to culture, amplify, and/or otherwise expand the harvest tissue prior to its deposit in the patient. 78. The system according to claim 77, wherein the tissue expansion kit comprises tissue culture medium. 79. The system according to claim 78, wherein the tissue culture medium comprises basal medium. 80. The system according to claim 78, wherein the tissue culture medium comprises growth medium selected from the group consisting of: DMEM/F12; lOmM HEPES; 2mM GlutaMax; l% PenStrep; and combinations thereof. 81. The system according to claim 78, wherein the tissue culture medium comprises an additive selected from the group consisting of: a tropic factor; a temporary additive; and combinations thereof. 82. The system according to claim 77, wherein the tissue expansion kit comprises a growth factor selected from the group consisting of: Gastrin 1, such as at a concentration of 10 nM; N Acetylcysteine (NAC), such as at a concentration of 1 mM; B27 supplement, such as at a concentration of 2% v/v; Wnt3A, such as at a concentration of 100 ng/mL; R- spondin 1, such as at a concentration of l pg/mL; Noggin, such as at a concentration of 100 ng/mL; epidermal growth factor (EGF), such as at a concentration of 50 ng/mL; A83- 01, such as at a concentration of 500 nM; SB202190 (p38 MAP kinase); and 

 combinations thereof. 83. The system according to claim 77, wherein the tissue expansion kit comprises a temporary additive, such as Y-27632 (ROCK inhibitor) and/or CHIR99021 (GSK-3 inhibitor), wherein the additive is added to the tissue at the onset of culturing and then removed after a limited time period. 84. The system according to claim 77, wherein the tissue expansion kit comprises a putative growth factor. 85. The system according to claim 84, wherein the putative growth factor includes a native molecule. 86. The system according to claim 84, wherein the putative growth factor includes an analog of a substance selected from the group consisting of: insulin; gastrin; betacellulin; amphiregulin; TGF-alpha: transforming growth factor-alpha; epidermal growth factor (EGF); heparin binding epidermal growth factor (HB-EGF); GLP-l : GLP-2; growth hormone; insulin-like growth factor- 1 (IGF-1); granulocyte colony stimulating factor (G- CSF); erythropoietin (EPO); intestinal trefoil factor (ITF); keratinocyte growth factor (KGF); hepatocyte growth factor (HGF); neuregulin-4 (NRG-4); and combinations thereof. 87. The system according to claim 77, wherein the tissue expansion kit includes one or more support structures configured to support organoid growth of the material. 88. The system according to claim 87, wherein the support structure is selected from the group consisting of: a basement membrane matrix comprising a gelatinous protein mixture, such as Matrigel; basement membrane extracts (BMEs); a PEGylated hydrogel; a hydrogel with tunable elasticity, such to provide beneficial effects on cell proliferation and differentiation; and combinations thereof. 89. The system as claimed in at least one of the preceding claims, further comprising a tissue modification kit including one or more components configured to modify the tissue and/or material prior to its deposit in the patient. 90. The system according to claim 89, wherein the tissue modification kit is configured to genetically modify the tissue and/or material. 91. The system according to claim 89, wherein the tissue modification kit comprises a gene delivery medium comprising a mechanism selected from the group consisting of: transposon, such as a PiggyBac transposon; viral vector, such as retrovirus, lentivirus, adenovirus, or adeno-associated virus, CRISPR-Cas9; electroporation and/or sonoporation mechanism; Lipofection; and combinations thereof. 92. The system according to claim 89, wherein the tissue modification kit is configured to perform a genetic modification selected from the group consisting of: geneâknock-outâ, such that a gene is made inoperative; geneâknock-inâ, such that a gene or portion thereof is substituted for another gene or portion thereof; modification of noncoding portions of the genome, such as promoters; insertion of novel genes, such as native or non-native genes; insertion of promoters; insertion of DNA; and combinations thereof. 93. The system according to claim 89, wherein the tissue modification kit includes a piece of genetic material configured to cause cell death when a specific nutrient is delivered to the cell. 94. The system according to claim 89, wherein the tissue modification kit includes a piece of genetic material configured to cause cell death when the cell is denied a specific nutrient. 95. The system as claimed in at least one of the preceding claims, further comprising a cell sorting kit including one or more equipment, materials, and/or components configured to sort cells of the tissue and/or material prior to its deposit in the patient. 96. The system according to claim 95, wherein the cell sorting kit is configured to perform a cell sorting process using one or more processes selected from the group consisting of: presence of cell surface antigens, such as Lgr5, and such as coupled with FACS and/or MACS; detection of proteins via intracellular staining coupled with flow cytometry, such as when only cells with intracellular GIP are selected; selection by application of an antibiotic such as puromycin, such as when the transduced cells have antibiotic resistance imparted as part of a genetic modification that has been performed; density gradient 

 separation, such as centrifugation, and such as following a dissociation and/or trypsinization step; filtering through a porous membrane with a particular pore size, such as an approximately 70 micron pore size, and such as following a dissociation and/or trypsinization step; and combinations thereof. 97. The system as claimed in at least one of the preceding claims, further comprising a deposit material assembly kit including one or more equipment, materials, containers, and/or components configured to assemble the material prior to its deposit in the patient. 98. The system according to claim 97, wherein the deposit material assembly kit includes one or more preservatives, such as cryopreservative. 99. The system according to claim 97, wherein the deposit material assembly kit includes one or more antibiotics, such as penicillin and/or puromycin. 100. The system according to claim 99, wherein the material is genetically modified to be antibiotic resistant and the antibiotic is added to the material to provide an inherent growth advantage over native cells at the deposit site, wherein the native cells are not antibiotic resistant. 101. The system as claimed in at least one of the preceding claims, further comprising an expansion device configured to deliver injectate into tissue at the harvesting and/or deposit site. 102. The system as claimed in at least one of the preceding claims, further comprising a first treatment device configured to ablate and/or remove tissue at or otherwise proximate the deposit site. 103. The system according to claim 102, further comprising a second treatment device configured to perform a tissue expansion procedure at or otherwise proximate the deposit site. 104. The system according to claim 103, wherein the first treatment device and the second treatment device are the same device. 105. The system according to claim 104, wherein the depositing device, the first treatment device, and the second treatment device are the same device. 106 The system as claimed in at least one of the preceding claims, wherein the system is configured to treat a medical condition selected from the group consisting of: Type 2 diabetes; Type 1 diabetes; Double diabetes; gestational diabetes; hyperglycemia; pre diabetes; monogenic diabetes; maturity onset diabetes of the young; impaired glucose tolerance; insulin resistance; hyperinsulinemia; hypoinsulinemia; non-diabetic hypoglycemia; elevated albuminuria; non-alcoholic fatty liver disease; non-alcoholic steatohepatitis; obesity; obesity-related disorder; polycystic ovarian syndrome; hypertriglyceridemia; hypercholesterolemia; psoriasis; gastroesophageal reflux disease; coronary artery disease; stroke; transient ischemic attack; cognitive decline; dementia; Alzheimer's Disease; neuropathy; diabetic nephropathy; retinopathy; heart disease; diabetic heart disease; heart failure; diabetic heart failure; hirsutism; hyperandrogenism; fertility issues; menstrual dysfunction; cancer; liver cancer; ovarian cancer; breast cancer; endometrial cancer; cholangiocarcinoma; adenocarcinoma; glandular tissue tumor; stomach cancer; colorectal cancer; prostate cancer; diastolic dysfunction; hypertension; myocardial infarction; microvascular disease related to diabetes; anorexia nervosa; anorexia; a binge eating disorder; a hyperphagic state; hyperphagia; polyphagia; Prader Willi syndrome; an obesity-related genetic disorder; hypoglycemia; hypoglycemia that presents after a bariatric procedure (referred to asâpost-bariatric hypoglycemiaâ);
